case: standalone preposition governs the following noun in the genitive, ending visible
reason: layer-1-safe prep->genitive resolves ONLY because the kasra is visible; still iʿrāb-gated, never auto_safe

case: same preposition + noun but unvoweled / arbitrary typing
reason: the ending is not visible, so even the safe rule stays a candidate (gate >= two_vote), never resolved

case: prepositional phrase head is undecidable from the surface
reason: which head إلى المدرسة attaches to (verb / nominal / hidden hal) stays UNRESOLVED unless evidence justifies it
keep muḍāf-ilayh (genitive) / nominal-sentence khabar (nominative) / ṣifa / badal as alternatives; assert a case only when the ending is visible

case: coordinating waw has no single governor
reason: a coordinating wāw is headless; never invent a governor for it

case: a tempting iʿrāb correction with no governor justification
reason: an iʿrāb correction is NEVER auto_safe without a stated governor; prefer abstain/reject over a wrong fix

case: CEFR depth gate over the same edge
cefr_note: A1/A2 avoid iʿrāb terminology; C1/C2 may show the governor and the case it assigns
reason: CEFR gates how much iʿrāb metalanguage is exposed, never the linguistics; the lattice is unchanged by level
